inline_comments.message: I definitely don't know this code base but locking might cause a bottle neck.  Maybe instead of saving a single state, you could use a (limited sized) stack and simply push in the latest state snapshot onto the stack?  Pushing and popping off the stack is atomic so no lock needed.  Just a suggestion.

thank you for the suggestion.

there is not much to bottle neck here - the reconfigure event happens very rarely - like every 20 minutes or so - just need not to step over the current policy-update event if any.  

The periodic catch_up will solve all the lost and missed communication issues eventually anyway.
